pubmed_links: [Alex et al. (2025) GWAS for behavioral traits in golden retrievers identifies genes implicated in human temperament, mental health, and cognition](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41284867/);[Yang et al. (2025) Machine learning-driven transcriptomic study reveals the key role of Romo1 in reversing central sensitization through stellate ganglion block in migraine](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40747904/)
summary: ROMO1 (Reactive Oxygen Species Modulator 1), a mitochondrial inner membrane protein involved in redox signaling and mitochondrial dynamics, has been identified within a genome-wide significant locus for cognitive trainability in dogs and associated with intelligence, depression, irritability, and emotional sensitivity in humans via cross-species GWAS; it has also been implicated in central sensitization mechanisms in migraine through transcriptomic analysis. Direct evidence in classic neuropsychiatric or neurodevelopmental disorders such as autism or schizophrenia remains absent. (novel candidate)
